evidence: QueryExecutor interface: all methods have Javadoc with @param/@return/@throws, consistent (String, String, double, int) parameter ordering for collocation methods
HttpApiUtils: 3 static helpers with uniform signatures — all complete public-API helpers with no hidden error contracts
Handler methods uniformly follow (HttpExchange exchange) + throws IOException signature throughout WordSketchApiServer

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern() has 10 parameters: seed, topPredicates, nounsPerPredicate, minShared, minLogDice, bcqlPattern, simplePattern, relationName, headPos, collocatePos — a clear API boundary violation

Single-seed handler delegates to SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern(); multi-seed handler (handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti) reimplements the collocate-intersection logic inline, bypassing SemanticFieldExplorer entirely — asymmetric design

Explore endpoint uses ?seed= (singular) and defaults min_logdice=3.0; explore-multi uses ?seeds= and defaults min_logdice=2.0; nouns_per param exists only on single-seed endpoint — inconsistent parameter vocabulary between sibling endpoints

findDependencyCollocations() exists on BlackLabQueryExecutor as a concrete public method but is absent from the QueryExecutor interface; DEP handlers call executeSurfacePattern instead, leaking a dual-path into the implementation
PatternSubstitution.extractXposFromConstraint() and escapeForRegex() are public but undocumented and carry no error contract
executeSurfacePattern interface method has 6 parameters, just above the 5-parameter threshold
handleConcordanceExamples manually strips & after parseQueryParams already ran URLDecoder — redundant and inconsistent decode path

strengths: QueryExecutor interface is well-documented with @param/@return/@throws on every method, and default methods provide explicit backward-compatibility contracts
HttpApiUtils static helpers (sendJsonResponse, sendError, parseQueryParams) have a clean, narrow shape with no overloading
HTTP handler methods use a uniform (HttpExchange exchange) throws IOException signature, and error responses consistently delegate to HttpApiUtils.sendError()
WordSketchApiServer constructor is honest and explicit: 4 clearly named dependencies, no hidden state
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: Issues cluster around the semantic-field subsystem: the explore vs explore-multi pair is the primary offender, with an asymmetric delegation pattern, inconsistent query-parameter vocabulary, and a 10-parameter public method — the rest of the API surface is largely coherent.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The QueryExecutor interface and HttpApiUtils layer score well — documented, consistently shaped, honest error contracts. However, the semantic-field API introduces several real coherence problems: the 10-parameter exploreByPattern() is a clear API boundary failure, single-seed and multi-seed endpoints share a conceptual purpose but differ in parameter names and defaults, and the multi-seed handler reimplements business logic instead of delegating. These are not cosmetic inconsistencies; they create friction for any developer trying to extend or test the semantic-field subsystem. At the 80-range anchor ('solid but uneven'), the good infrastructure pieces offset the worst offenders, but the asymmetries and the parameter-count violation pull the score meaningfully below that, landing around 54.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern() has 10 parameters, far exceeding the readable-API threshold
evidence: Method signature: exploreByPattern(String seed, int topPredicates, int nounsPerPredicate, int minShared, double minLogDice, String bcqlPattern, String simplePattern, String relationName, int headPos, int collocatePos)
All 10 arguments are positional with no named structure; callers in WordSketchApiServer.java construct them inline from 7+ local variables
issues.suggestion: Introduce an ExplorationConfig value object (or record) grouping bcqlPattern, simplePattern, relationName, headPos, collocatePos, minLogDice, and the limit parameters; reduce exploreByPattern to (String seed, ExplorationConfig config)

issues.summary: Multi-seed handler bypasses SemanticFieldExplorer and reimplements collocate-intersection logic inline
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore() delegates to explorer.exploreByPattern(); handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti() builds seedToCollocates + commonCollocates directly, never calling SemanticFieldExplorer
SemanticFieldExplorer has an exploreMulti-style comparison capability (compare()) that is not used here
issues.suggestion: Add an exploreMulti(Set<String> seeds, ExplorationConfig config) method to SemanticFieldExplorer and delegate from handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti; keep the HTTP handler thin

issues.summary: Sibling explore and explore-multi endpoints use different parameter names, defaults, and available options
evidence: explore: ?seed= (singular), min_logdice default 3.0, nouns_per param supported
explore-multi: ?seeds= (plural), min_logdice default 2.0, no nouns_per param
issues.suggestion: Unify parameter names: use seeds= for both (accept single value as a one-element set), standardise defaults to the same value, and support the same optional params on both endpoints

issues.summary: findDependencyCollocations() is a public method on BlackLabQueryExecutor but absent from QueryExecutor interface
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor.findDependencyCollocations(String headword, String deprel, double minLogDice, int maxResults) is public but not declared in QueryExecutor
DEP relation handlers reach it indirectly through executeSurfacePattern, not through a named dependency-aware method
issues.suggestion: Add findDependencyCollocations(String headword, String deprel, double minLogDice, int maxResults) as a default method on QueryExecutor (throwing UnsupportedOperationException by default) so that DEP-aware callers can type-check or dispatch properly

issues.summary: handleConcordanceExamples decodes & manually after parseQueryParams already ran URLDecoder
evidence: Lines: `if (word1 != null) word1 = word1.replace("&", "&");` applied after HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams() has already URLDecoded the value
No other handler applies this secondary decode
issues.suggestion: Remove the manual & stripping; if HTML-entity-encoded URLs are a real edge case, handle it centrally in parseQueryParams

root_causes: The semantic-field subsystem was added incrementally (single-seed first, then multi-seed) without a shared abstraction to anchor both paths
SemanticFieldExplorer was designed as a stateful exploration engine but its public method grew to accept all caller-side config inline rather than through a config object
likely_symptoms: explore_multi_inline_logic — duplicated intersection code is likely a symptom of missing multi-seed method on SemanticFieldExplorer
explore_vs_exploremulti_param_inconsistency — likely a symptom of the two handlers being written in separate increments
